What's the benefit of taking out the entire lung rather than just the tissue?
Good question.
So this is based on several studies and research that it's not just cancer has a tendency, it's just the tip of the iceberg what you see.
So the nodule is the tip of the iceberg.
It tends to spread through small vessels and airways.
The cancer cells spread through all this and get to lymph nodes and other parts of the body.
So when you do a lobectomy and take out all these lymph nodes and the whole section that goes with it, then basically you're getting a good margin around it.
And so that gives the patient a best chance of overall survival and cancer-free survival.
So the cancer coming back is smaller, the chances are smaller, rather than if you just take just the piece of lung.
